response:
Treating this as a fresh PR with no baseline. Verdicts grouped by category; "sev / ar" = `scores.severity` / `scores.agent_risk`.

## Block merge (5)

These either break on any machine but the author's, or silently produce wrong output that tests can't catch.

- **crime_00007 — Localhost-on-Disk** (sev 0.6, ar 0.53). `src/api/files.ts:9` ships the literal `/Users/andrew/dev/crimes/schema.json`. Code is broken on every other developer's machine, every CI runner, every deploy. Severity understates this — it's a guaranteed runtime failure, not a smell.
- **crime_00006 — Dev-Server URL** (sev 0.6, ar 0.53). `localhost:3000` baked into `src/api/files.ts:8`. Same logic as 00007: works nowhere except the author's local stack.
- **crime_00004 — Half-UTC, Half-Local** (sev 0.8, ar 0.61). Same `Date` receiver mixes `getUTCFullYear()` and `getMonth()` (`src/date.ts:9,41`). Silent bug by host UTC offset — the kind of issue that ships green and fails in prod for non-UTC users.
- **crime_00010 — Timezone Roulette** (sev 0.6, ar 0.53). `new Date("2026-12-25T07:00:00")` and `new Date("2026-12-20")` in `src/date.ts`. Christmas Day swings by 24h depending on host TZ. Trivial fix, high-confidence wrongness — block.
- **crime_00003 — Temporal Recklessness in src/date.ts** (sev 0.8, ar 0.62). 8 raw `Date.now()`/`new Date()` calls in what is clearly the date-handling module. Together with 00004/00010 this whole file needs a rewrite before merge — clock injection isn't optional for the module that *is* the clock.

## Warn (16)

Real problems, worth fixing soon, but not gating on a hypothetical green light. Each carries enough severity or agent_risk to surface in review but none are immediate correctness failures.

- **crime_00002 — God Function `generateInvoice`** (sev 0.9, ar 0.55). 204 lines is genuinely scary, but it's structural — not a runtime defect. Split into a follow-up.
- **crime_00009 — Temporal Recklessness in billing.ts** (sev 0.6, ar 0.46). 7 more direct `Date` calls. Same family as 00003 but lower severity; flag for the same rewrite.
- **crime_00014 — DST-Naive Day Math** (sev 0.6, ar 0.52). `+ 86400000` in date code. Pair with the clock-injection fix.
- **crime_00001 — SVG With Embedded Raster** (sev 0.75, ar 0.7). High severity score, but it's an asset hygiene issue — visual works, just inflates bytes. Warn.
- **crime_00005 — Missing Agent Context** (sev 0.6, ar 0.53). No AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / SKILL.md. Reviewer-visible omission that this scan itself argues matters; not a code defect.
- **crime_00018 — Duplicated Navigation Source** (sev 0.6, ar 0.49). `/settings/billing` labelled "Plans" in registry, "Billing" in sidebar. A user will see drift; fix before customers do.
- **crime_00020 — Route Metadata Drift** (sev 0.6, ar 0.49). Same route is "Plans", "Subscription", "Billing", and `PricingPage` across path/title/nav. Pair with 00018.
- **crime_00012 — String Sprinkles "enterprise"** (sev 0.5, ar 0.52). Same domain literal across 4 areas. About to become duplicated policy; centralise now while there are only four call sites.
- **crime_00015 — Concept Alias Drift (tenant/account/org/team/workspace)** (sev 0.55, ar 0.58). Highest `agent_risk` in the medium tier — exactly the kind of vocabulary drift that makes agents add a sixth term instead of finding the existing one. Document the canonical noun in AGENTS.md (resolves 00005 partially) before adding more.
- **crime_00013 — `calculateRefundPreview` False Identity** (sev 0.55, ar 0.44). `calculate*` name + `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` inside. Rename or extract — pure-looking names that mutate state get re-called by accident.
- **crime_00016 — God File billing.ts (310 lines)** (sev 0.6, ar 0.42). Barely over threshold. Warn but don't block — folding it in with the 00002 split is the cheaper path.
- **crime_00017 — Logic in the Alibi** (sev 0.5, ar 0.42). Comment encodes the owner-only refund rule; nearby code doesn't. The exact "rules in prose" risk this scan flags. Encode in a guard.
- **crime_00011 — Test That Proves Nothing (no asserts)** (sev 0.45, ar 0.46). A `test("renders the enterprise plan")` with zero `expect` is false confidence. Either assert or delete.
- **crime_00019 — Unfinished Business (`src/todo.ts`, 14 markers)** (sev 0.4, ar 0.49). 424 TODOs/1k LOC. The file is unreviewable as intent — triage or document which markers are load-bearing.
- **crime_00023 — Sync I/O in Hot Path** (sev 0.4, ar 0.44). `existsSync`/`statSync`/`readFileSync` in `loadPlanSchema`. Fine for a CLI bin, bad in an API handler. File path is `src/api/files.ts` — warn and confirm context with author.
- **crime_00021 — Oversized Raster (253 KB hero)** (sev 0.4, ar 0.52). Just over the 200 KB low threshold. Worth a resize but not gating.

## Ignore (16)

Each has sev ≤ 0.4 *and* ar ≤ 0.52, and none is a correctness defect. Leaving them on the floor is the right move — surfacing all of these in PR review would train the team to ignore the tool.

- **crime_00022 — Docs-Code Drift** (sev 0.35, ar 0.51). One missing `./setup.md` link in `docs/teams.md`. Fix when touching docs.
- **crime_00024 — Temporal Recklessness in state.ts** (sev 0.35, ar 0.44). One `Date.now()`. Roll into the broader clock-injection follow-up.
- **crime_00025 — Icon-Sized Raster (32×32 PNG)** (sev 0.35, ar 0.5). Aesthetic; not blocking.
- **crime_00026 — Date String Sewing** (sev 0.3, ar 0.43). One `+ getUTCFullYear()` concat. Part of the date.ts cleanup.
- **crime_00027 — Host-Locale Drift** (sev 0.3, ar 0.43). One `toLocaleDateString()` with no arg. Same cleanup.
- **crime_00028 — Unprefixed Boolean (state.ts)** (sev 0.3, ar 0.42). Naming nit.
- **crime_00029 — Unprefixed Boolean (petty.ts)** (sev 0.3, ar 0.42). Same.
- **crime_00030 — Test With Weak Matcher** (sev 0.25, ar 0.34). Lowest agent_risk in the report — true noise.
- **crime_00031 — Option Bag Junk Drawer** (sev 0.3, ar 0.4). Refactor candidate.
- **crime_00032 — Return Shape Roulette** (sev 0.3, ar 0.4). Same.
- **crime_00033 — Negative Flag Maze** (sev 0.25, ar 0.4). Two negative flags. Cosmetic.
- **crime_00034 — Plural Mismatch** (sev 0.3, ar 0.4). `users: User`, `invoice: Invoice[]`. Worth a sweep eventually; v1 detector by the tool's own note.
- **crime_00035 — Unfinished Business (billing.ts, 12.9/1k LOC)** (sev 0.2, ar 0.32). Barely over threshold; superseded by 00019.
- **crime_00036 — Orphaned `/account/subscription`** (sev 0.4, ar 0.39).
- **crime_00037 — Orphaned `/team`** (sev 0.4, ar 0.39).
- **crime_00038 — Orphaned `/workspace/members`** (sev 0.4, ar 0.39). All three orphaned routes are likely in-flight; ignore until the journey lands or doesn't.

## Roll-up

- **5 block** (date wrongness × 3, machine-specific paths × 2)
- **16 warn** (structure, naming, drift — non-trivial follow-ups)
- **16 ignore** (cosmetic / refactor candidates)

The signal-to-noise here matches the tool's `agent_risk` ranking better than raw `severity`: the two "high severity" structural findings (00001, 00002) are warn-tier, and a few "medium" findings (00004, 00010) earn block status because their wrongness is silent and certain.
